name: T3 ALMA Smooth Crossover
edge_hypothesis: T3 (Tillson triple-smoothed EMA with volume factor 0.7) and ALMA (Arnaud Legoux with Gaussian offset 6.0 and sigma 0.85) are mathematically distinct smoothing approaches that have NEVER been tested in any strategy. T3 prioritizes lag reduction through triple exponential smoothing with a volume factor that controls overshoot. ALMA applies a Gaussian distribution centered on recent prices, rejecting outlier noise without the lag penalty of longer EMAs. When these two advanced MAs cross, it should produce fewer false signals than EMA_9/EMA_21 crossovers because both independently filter noise through fundamentally different mathematical mechanisms. The key test: does superior smoothing math translate to superior trading signals? Stochastic confirmation (STOCHk < 50 for longs, > 50 for shorts) ensures momentum alignment without being restrictive.

variants.name: t3_alma_stoch_v1_8to1
variants.description: T3 crosses above ALMA with Stochastic confirmation that momentum isn't already extended. No explicit regime gate — testing raw signal quality of smooth crossover across all regimes. 8:1 R:R.
filters: T3/ALMA smooth crossover: T3_10_0.7 crosses ALMA_9_6.0_0.85
Stochastic momentum: STOCHk_14_3_3 confirms direction room
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: T3_10_0.7 — triple-smoothed EMA with volume factor 0.7. Low lag, smooth response. NEVER tested.
ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 — Gaussian-weighted MA. Noise rejection via distribution weighting. NEVER tested.
STOCHk_14_3_3 < 50 (long) / > 50 (short). Confirms momentum has room to run.

exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).

components.notes: T3_10_0.7 — triple-smoothed EMA. Faster crossover line.
CHOP_14_1_100 > 50 = ranging gate. Tests if smooth crossover benefits from regime filtering.

variants.name: t3_alma_vortex_v3_8to1
variants.description: T3/ALMA crossover with Vortex directional confirmation instead of Stochastic. Tests whether combining smooth-MA crossover with our best directional indicator (Vortex) creates synergy. Vortex confirms the crossover direction is backed by genuine directional momentum.
filters: T3/ALMA smooth crossover: T3_10_0.7 crosses ALMA_9_6.0_0.85
Vortex directional: VTXP_14 vs VTXM_14 confirms direction
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).

R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.

components.notes: T3_10_0.7 — triple-smoothed EMA. Faster crossover signal.
VTXP_14 > VTXM_14 (long) / VTXM_14 > VTXP_14 (short). Proven directional indicator — confirms crossover direction.

indicator_roles.baseline: T3_10_0.7 / ALMA_9_6.0_0.85 smooth crossover (both NEVER tested)
indicator_roles.confirmation: STOCHk (v1) / CHOP (v2) / Vortex (v3) — three different confirmation philosophies
